channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: In Memoriam: Those we lost in 2024
item.description: They came from every walk of life to make ours better, richer, more rounded. Here's a look at those we lost in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter is being mourned in his tiny hometown and around the world
item.description: PLAINS, Ga. (AP) — Johnny Jones found out about Jimmy Carter's death within a matter of minutes. That's how it works in a small town, even for a former U.S. president and Nobel Peace Prize winner known throughout the world.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, SHARON JOHNSON and BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Pentagon chief loses bid to reject 9/11 plea deals
item.description: A military appeals court has ruled against Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin's effort to throw out the plea deals reached for Khalid Sheikh Mohammed and two other defendants in the 9/11 attacks.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Speaker math: Breaking down how many votes Johnson needs to keep his gavel
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) heads to the House floor on Friday in hopes of being formally reelected Speaker. While Johnson has the endorsement of President-elect Trump to remain Speaker, one Republican is pledging to oppose him, and several others are not committing to supporting Johnson.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Delphi murders: Judge rules autopsy photos, records be sealed
item.description: Judge Fran Gull ruled that all crime scene and autopsy photos - as well as medical and mental health records - be sealed.
item.dc:creator: Matt Adams, NewsNation

item.title: Cities across the globe prepare to welcome the New Year in celebrations tinged with local traditions
item.description: Cities around the world are readying to ring in the New Year with celebrations highlighting local cultures and traditions, after a year roiled by ongoing conflict and political instability.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Connecticut man allegedly killed a mother, her infant son over $400 she owed him for renting car
item.description: A Connecticut man who allegedly killed a woman and her infant son in November targeted the woman because she owed him $400 for renting a vehicle of his, arrest reports said on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas teen allegedly killed rival competitor's show goat in act of jealousy
item.description: A Texas teenager is facing a felony cruelty to animals charge after she allegedly poisoned a rival competitor's 6-month-old show goat, resulting in his death.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AP WAS THERE: Carter announces Middle East peace agreement
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — ___ EDITORS: As part of its coverage of the death of former President Jimmy Carter, The Associated Press is looking back at major events in his life. Carter's success at Camp David negotiating a Mideast Peace agreement between Egypt and Israel was one of his crowning achievements.
item.dc:creator: BARRY SCHWEID, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: AP WAS THERE: Carter announces Middle East peace agreement
item.description: One of Jimmy Carter's crowning achievements as president was the Camp David talks that would deliver Israel's first peace agreement with an Arab nation and make Nobel Peace Prize winners of Egyptian President Anwar Sadat and Israeli Prime Minister Menachem Begin.
item.dc:creator: Barry Schweid, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Migrant crisis added to record-level US homelessness: HUD
item.description: Sanctuary cities saw homelessness rates jump thanks in part to a surge of migrants being sent from the southern border.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge denies sentencing delay for ex-Sen. Bob Menendez but puts his wife's trial off until February
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former New Jersey Sen. Bob Menendez will be sentenced on corruption charges as scheduled at the end of January, and his wife’s trial will be moved from January to February, a judge said Monday. U.S. District Judge Sidney H.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FBI issues warning after home of NBA star Luka Doncic targeted in string of burglaries
item.description: The FBI is warning sports leagues about crime organizations targeting professional athletes following a string of burglaries at the homes of prominent NFL and NBA players.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, KRON4

item.title: Times Square ball takes final test for New Year's Eve
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The crystal-covered ball that descends down a pole in Times Square to ring in the new year was taken for a test run Monday, as New York City officials laid out their plans for the iconic New Year's Eve event.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Move over, Gen Alpha: 2025 marks the start of the Gen Beta era
item.description: If you just found out about Generation Alpha, brace yourself for a new crop of kids set to debut on Jan. 1.
item.dc:creator: Sakshi Venkatraman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cats can get sick with bird flu. Here's how to protect them
item.description: The death of an Oregon house cat and a pet food recall are raising questions about the ongoing outbreak of bird flu and how people can protect their pets.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Northern lights could be visible in upper fringes of the US on New Year's Eve
item.description: There's a chance solar storms may bring northern lights to several northern U.S states just in time for the new year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hackers have posted some RIBridges data on the dark web, McKee says
item.description: The state disclosed earlier this month that cybercriminals had gained access to the state's IT system for social services.
item.dc:creator: Ted Nesi, NewsNation

item.title: Keanu Reeves’ stolen watches located in Chile: reports
item.description: Stolen luxury watches belonging to Keanu Reeves, including a Rolex worth almost $10,000, have been found far from where they were stolen.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: South Korea struggles to find cause of plane crash that killed 179
item.description: South Korean officials said Monday they will conduct safety inspections of all Boeing 737-800 aircraft operated by the country's airlines as they struggle to determine what caused a plane crash that killed 179 people a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Washington inmate accused of molesting cellmate after changing gender, transfer to women's prison
item.description: A former Washington inmate is suing the state and prison officials after she says they forced her into a cell with a biological male who molested her.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Appeals court upholds verdict finding Trump liable for sexually assaulting E. Jean Carroll
item.description: A federal appeals court panel on Monday upheld a jury’s verdict finding President-elect Donald Trump liable for sexually assaulting advice columnist E. Jean Carroll and ordering him to pay $5 million.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: LeBron James at 40: A milestone birthday arrives Monday for the NBA's all-time scoring leader
item.description: When LeBron James broke another NBA record earlier this month, the one for most regular-season minutes played in a career, his Los Angeles Lakers teammates handled the moment in typical locker room fashion. They made fun of him. “They told me I’m old as hell,” James quipped.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Florida law will create safety barrier for first responders
item.description: A new Florida law will keep first responders safe by prohibiting a person from approaching any police, fire or EMS personnel while working on a call.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida man allegedly attempted to 'club' wife, stab himself in the heart on Christmas Day
item.description: A Florida man allegedly attempted to murder his wife then commit suicide on Christmas Day in their home, according to police reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jetliner skids off runway and bursts into flames while landing in South Korea, killing 179
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A jetliner skidded off a runway, slammed into a concrete fence and burst into flames Sunday in South Korea after its landing gear apparently failed to deploy. All but two of the 181 people aboard were killed in one of the country’s worst aviation disasters, officials said.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Charles Shyer, ‘Father of the Bride’ and ‘Baby Boom’ filmmaker, dies at 83
item.description: Charles Shyer, the Oscar-nominated writer and filmmaker known for classic comedies like “Private Benjamin,” “Baby Boom” and “Father of the Bride” that he made alongside Nancy Meyers, has died. He was 83.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Carter to be honored with events in Georgia, Washington
item.description: Former President Carter will be honored with public observances in both Georgia and Washington, D.C., in the coming days, following his death on Sunday. He will be interred in Plains, Ga., according to the Carter Center.
item.dc:creator: Katie Wadington, WGN 9

item.title: Photos: Jimmy Carter, from the White House to building houses
item.description: From The White House to building houses, here's a look at former president Jimmy Carter's life in photos.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Five defining moments of Jimmy Carter's presidency
item.description: Former President Carter's post-presidential life lasted decades, putting his career in the White House in a deep rearview mirror in many ways. Carter died Sunday, according to the Carter Center.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Neukam, WGN 9

item.title: Tornadoes in Texas and Mississippi kill 2 and injure 6 as severe weather system moves east
item.description: A strong storm system threatened to whip up tornadoes in parts of the U.S. Southeast on Sunday, a day after severe weather claimed at least two lives as twisters touched down in Texas and Mississippi.
item.dc:creator: ABC 4

item.title: Dodgers' Ohtani and wife will soon be welcoming a baby to the family
item.description: Between a marriage announcement, third MVP award and World Series title, Los Angeles Dodgers superstar Shohei Ohtani had a pretty good 2024. And now, it looks like 2025 may be even better for the Japanese two-way baseball phenom.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5
